headline: GST Reintroduction Debate Returns With 4% Proposal
context: Economy Minister Rafizi Ramli hinted in February 2026 that a revised GST at 4% could replace SST to broaden the tax base, sparking fierce debate among coalition partners.

cards.big: GST at 4% could raise RM45 billion yearly — nearly double SST's RM28 billion collection
cards.sub: But Malaysians remember the chaos of 2015 GST rollout and price spikes that followed

cards.big: Malaysia's tax-to-GDP ratio is 11.8% — lowest in ASEAN after Myanmar's 7.5%
cards.sub: Thailand collects 16.5% and Indonesia 10.9% — both with broader consumption taxes

cards.big: 2015 GST rollout caused 3-month consumer price spike of 4.2% hitting low-income hardest
cards.sub: Zero-rated essentials covered only 545 items — daily necessities still saw price creep

cards.big: Royal Malaysian Customs lost 2,800 trained GST officers after 2018 abolition disrupted operations
cards.sub: Rebuilding administrative capacity could take 18-24 months and RM600 million investment

cards.big: The question isn't whether Malaysia needs GST — it's whether politicians will spend the extra revenue responsibly
Should GST revenue be legally ring-fenced for healthcare and education only?
